description_long: This object is a long, single-edged sword with a slightly curved blade, reminiscent of a traditional saber or scimitar. The blade is dark, possibly made of steel, and features a sharp, clean edge suitable for slicing. The handle is ergonomically curved, wrapped in a dark material with visible decorative studs or rivets, and ends in a gold-toned pommel. A prominent, ornate crossguard with gold accents separates the blade from the handle, providing both protection and visual distinction. The overall design suggests a blend of functionality and elegance, with the gold embellishments adding a touch of luxury. This sword would be visually striking in an urban display, collection, or as a prop in a themed environment.
description: A long, curved, single-edged sword with a dark blade, ornate gold crossguard, and a decorated, curved handle.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved blade, ornate crossguard, and detailed handle.
description_view_1: Edge-on view highlighting the thinness of the blade and the decorative, star-shaped crossguard.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the blade's curve and handle details, with gold accents visible.
description_view_3: Handle-end view showing the pommel, grip wrapping, and the symmetrical design of the crossguard.
attribute_handle_design: curved, wrapped handle with decorative studs and a gold pommel
attribute_ornamentation: gold accents and decorative studs on handle and guard
